target.writeBoundary: local README-only clarification; do not modify Vue source files, package scripts, dependency versions, tests, generated build output, or upstream repository state

userConfirmedIntent.summary: Use an actual mdn/todo-vue clone as a real external operation-chain dogfood and apply only a local README clarification about using dev/build commands after dependency installation.
includedBehavior: Clone the real external repository locally.
Recover high-level project intent from README and package.json.
Apply only a local README clarification.
Generate a graph delta and graph update proposal from the local diff.
excludedBehavior: Do not change src/App.vue or Vue component behavior.
Do not change package.json scripts or dependency versions.
Do not change tests or build configuration.
Do not create an upstream PR.
Do not claim maintainer approval or user acceptance.

observedAnchors.anchor: Sample todo app built with the Vue framework.
observedAnchors.meaning: The repository is a learning/sample Todo application, not a product where PBE can assume maintainer intent.
Dependency installation is the first setup action documented for local use.
The README documents the development server command.
The README documents the production build command.
The dev command maps to Vite.
The build command maps to Vite's production build.

candidateMatch.recommendedShape: Apply a README-only command-flow clarification in the local clone.
candidateMatch.whyThisShape: The selected external operation-chain dogfood should prove real external clone handling, graph-source instruction pack generation, local diff capture, and graph delta/update proposal creation without claiming maintainer intent or changing runtime behavior.
risksIfWrong: Changing Vue source behavior would exceed the selected README-only slice.
Changing package scripts or dependency versions could create unreviewed tooling drift.
Treating observed README/package.json intent as maintainer approval would violate retrofit truth boundaries.

implementationPlan.expectedFlow: Add only a README note that dependency installation precedes the local development server and production build check commands.
nonGoals: No test, lint, dependency, or build configuration change.
No upstream branch, issue, or pull request creation.

forbiddenFlows.reason: The selected dogfood is README-only and has no Product/AC approval for behavior changes.
The README guidance should align with existing scripts, not change tooling.
No maintainer review or upstream PR is part of this local operation-chain dogfood.

build.summary: npm ci passed, but npm run build failed before selected-slice validation could complete because the local Node.js v20.16.0 environment is below the project/tooling requirement and Rolldown native optional binding was unavailable. This is recorded as an external baseline/toolchain blocker, not as evidence that the README-only change changed runtime behavior.

runtime.summary: No runtime behavior is changed by this README-only dogfood.

hardware.summary: todo-vue has no hardware validation surface.

remainingRisks: The README clarification is local-only and not proposed upstream.
Build evidence is blocked by external baseline/toolchain requirements: Node 20.19+ or 22.12+ is required, while this local run used Node 20.16.0.
Any future source behavior change requires a separate graph-backed Product/Work/Test/Evidence slice.
nextAllowedActions: Inspect the graph delta before any graph-source update.
Do not modify source, package scripts, tests, or dependencies in this dogfood.
